vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or indistinguishable from soil debris. P2 and P4 are not clearly identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but shows signs of severe neglect or dormancy.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage consistent with String of Nickels.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage consistent with Mexican Mint.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos identified by structural remnants; White Rabbit anchor is present.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage consistent with Silver Guest.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No biological activity detected.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No biological activity detected.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Stressed. The Pothos shows minimal structural integrity. The White Rabbit anchor is the only clear feature.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No biological activity detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, potentially over-saturated or lacking organic aeration.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of a white, granular substance (possibly perlite or mineral salt buildup) on the surface of P3. A spoon-like object appears in T-3, suggesting human intervention/maintenance.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Minimal change; pots remain largely devoid of visible plant matter.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Introduction of a metallic tool (spoon) near the yellow pot, indicating an attempt at soil manipulation or feeding.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Return to baseline state; no significant growth or recovery observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of photosynthetic tissue across all pots suggests the specimens have either perished or are in a state of extreme, likely terminal, dormancy.
anomalies: Presence of white granular debris in P3.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the expected botanical specimens. The environment is currently a collection of soil-filled containers with no active, visible plant life. The Pothos (P3) is the only pot showing any structural remnants, though it is effectively non-viable. The presence of a spoon in the mid-sequence suggests an attempt to intervene, which has not resulted in any observable recovery or new growth.
